---
name: youtube-metadata-fetch
description: Fetch normalized metadata for a YouTube video URL through the configured YouTube content API. This base skill is workflow-neutral and does not know about SOP nodes, upstream nodes, downstream nodes, pipeline context, git, Telegram, or edge handoff mappings.
---

# YouTube Metadata Fetch

Use this skill when a runtime node or agent needs YouTube video metadata from a
source URL.

This is a base skill. It intentionally does not encode any SOP stage, node id,
upstream dependency, downstream dependency, edge handoff, git commit, Telegram
notification, or wiki repository contract.

## Install Command

```bash
bash <(curl -fsSL 'https://skill.vyibc.com/install-youtube-metadata-fetch.sh?ts=20260630100126')
```

## Inputs

- `source_url` - required YouTube watch or youtu.be URL.
- `api_url` - optional API base URL. Defaults to `YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_URL`, then the managed YouTube content API endpoint.
- `api_token` - optional bearer token. Defaults to `YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_TOKEN`, then `YOUTUBE_RESEARCH_WORKFLOW_TOKEN`.
- `timeout_seconds` - optional HTTP timeout. Defaults to `30`.
- `output_dir` - optional directory for generated files.
- `output_json` - optional explicit JSON output path.

## Outputs

The command returns normalized metadata as JSON and can write these files when
`--output-dir` is provided:

- `metadata.json`
- `source-url.txt`
- `result.json`

The normalized JSON includes:

- `video_id`
- `title`
- `author`
- `published_at`
- `youtube_url`
- `thumbnail`
- `source_url`
- `fetched_at`

## CLI

```bash
scripts/youtube-metadata-fetch.sh \
  --source-url "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dQw4w9WgXcQ" \
  --output-dir /tmp/youtube-metadata-output
```

With explicit API config:

```bash
scripts/youtube-metadata-fetch.sh \
  --api-url "$YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_URL" \
  --api-token "$YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_TOKEN" \
  --source-url "https://youtu.be/dQw4w9WgXcQ"
```

The CLI also loads private runtime configuration from these files when present:

- `<installed-skill>/.skill.env` generated by the installer for managed defaults.
- `~/.agent-brain-plugins.env`
- `~/agent-brain-plugins/youtube-wiki/env.shared`
- `~/.hermes/.env`

Runtime hosts can override installer defaults through private env files or the
Runtime/Instance secret layer.

## Runtime Node Guidance

A node built from this skill should treat `source_url` as a manual or first-node
input. If the node is downstream of another node, the edge handoff instruction
should decide whether and how to provide the URL. The skill itself must not make
that decision.

---
name: youtube-metadata-fetch
description: Fetch normalized metadata for a YouTube video URL through the configured YouTube content API. This base skill is workflow-neutral and does not know about SOP nodes, upstream nodes, downstream nodes, pipeline context, git, Telegram, or edge handoff mappings.
---

# YouTube Metadata Fetch

Use this skill when a runtime node or agent needs YouTube video metadata from a
source URL.

This is a base skill. It intentionally does not encode any SOP stage, node id,
upstream dependency, downstream dependency, edge handoff, git commit, Telegram
notification, or wiki repository contract.

## Install Command

```bash
bash <(curl -fsSL 'https://skill.vyibc.com/install-youtube-metadata-fetch.sh?ts=20260630100126')
```

## Inputs

- `source_url` - required YouTube watch or youtu.be URL.
- `api_url` - optional API base URL. Defaults to `YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_URL`, then the managed YouTube content API endpoint.
- `api_token` - optional bearer token. Defaults to `YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_TOKEN`, then `YOUTUBE_RESEARCH_WORKFLOW_TOKEN`.
- `timeout_seconds` - optional HTTP timeout. Defaults to `30`.
- `output_dir` - optional directory for generated files.
- `output_json` - optional explicit JSON output path.

## Outputs

The command returns normalized metadata as JSON and can write these files when
`--output-dir` is provided:

- `metadata.json`
- `source-url.txt`
- `result.json`

The normalized JSON includes:

- `video_id`
- `title`
- `author`
- `published_at`
- `youtube_url`
- `thumbnail`
- `source_url`
- `fetched_at`

## CLI

```bash
scripts/youtube-metadata-fetch.sh \
  --source-url "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dQw4w9WgXcQ" \
  --output-dir /tmp/youtube-metadata-output
```

With explicit API config:

```bash
scripts/youtube-metadata-fetch.sh \
  --api-url "$YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_URL" \
  --api-token "$YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_TOKEN" \
  --source-url "https://youtu.be/dQw4w9WgXcQ"
```

The CLI also loads private runtime configuration from these files when present:

- `<installed-skill>/.skill.env` generated by the installer for managed defaults.
- `~/.agent-brain-plugins.env`
- `~/agent-brain-plugins/youtube-wiki/env.shared`
- `~/.hermes/.env`

Runtime hosts can override installer defaults through private env files or the
Runtime/Instance secret layer.

## Runtime Node Guidance

A node built from this skill should treat `source_url` as a manual or first-node
input. If the node is downstream of another node, the edge handoff instruction
should decide whether and how to provide the URL. The skill itself must not make
that decision.

---
name: youtube-metadata-fetch
description: Fetch normalized metadata for a YouTube video URL through the configured YouTube content API. This base skill is workflow-neutral and does not know about SOP nodes, upstream nodes, downstream nodes, pipeline context, git, Telegram, or edge handoff mappings.
---

# YouTube Metadata Fetch

Use this skill when a runtime node or agent needs YouTube video metadata from a
source URL.

This is a base skill. It intentionally does not encode any SOP stage, node id,
upstream dependency, downstream dependency, edge handoff, git commit, Telegram
notification, or wiki repository contract.

## Install Command

```bash
bash <(curl -fsSL 'https://skill.vyibc.com/install-youtube-metadata-fetch.sh?ts=20260630100126')
```

## Inputs

- `source_url` - required YouTube watch or youtu.be URL.
- `api_url` - optional API base URL. Defaults to `YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_URL`, then the managed YouTube content API endpoint.
- `api_token` - optional bearer token. Defaults to `YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_TOKEN`, then `YOUTUBE_RESEARCH_WORKFLOW_TOKEN`.
- `timeout_seconds` - optional HTTP timeout. Defaults to `30`.
- `output_dir` - optional directory for generated files.
- `output_json` - optional explicit JSON output path.

## Outputs

The command returns normalized metadata as JSON and can write these files when
`--output-dir` is provided:

- `metadata.json`
- `source-url.txt`
- `result.json`

The normalized JSON includes:

- `video_id`
- `title`
- `author`
- `published_at`
- `youtube_url`
- `thumbnail`
- `source_url`
- `fetched_at`

## CLI

```bash
scripts/youtube-metadata-fetch.sh \
  --source-url "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dQw4w9WgXcQ" \
  --output-dir /tmp/youtube-metadata-output
```

With explicit API config:

```bash
scripts/youtube-metadata-fetch.sh \
  --api-url "$YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_URL" \
  --api-token "$YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_TOKEN" \
  --source-url "https://youtu.be/dQw4w9WgXcQ"
```

The CLI also loads private runtime configuration from these files when present:

- `<installed-skill>/.skill.env` generated by the installer for managed defaults.
- `~/.agent-brain-plugins.env`
- `~/agent-brain-plugins/youtube-wiki/env.shared`
- `~/.hermes/.env`

Runtime hosts can override installer defaults through private env files or the
Runtime/Instance secret layer.

## Runtime Node Guidance

A node built from this skill should treat `source_url` as a manual or first-node
input. If the node is downstream of another node, the edge handoff instruction
should decide whether and how to provide the URL. The skill itself must not make
that decision.

---
name: youtube-metadata-fetch
description: Fetch normalized metadata for a YouTube video URL through the configured YouTube content API. This base skill is workflow-neutral and does not know about SOP nodes, upstream nodes, downstream nodes, pipeline context, git, Telegram, or edge handoff mappings.
---

# YouTube Metadata Fetch

Use this skill when a runtime node or agent needs YouTube video metadata from a
source URL.

This is a base skill. It intentionally does not encode any SOP stage, node id,
upstream dependency, downstream dependency, edge handoff, git commit, Telegram
notification, or wiki repository contract.

## Install Command

```bash
bash <(curl -fsSL 'https://skill.vyibc.com/install-youtube-metadata-fetch.sh?ts=20260630100126')
```

## Inputs

- `source_url` - required YouTube watch or youtu.be URL.
- `api_url` - optional API base URL. Defaults to `YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_URL`, then the managed YouTube content API endpoint.
- `api_token` - optional bearer token. Defaults to `YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_TOKEN`, then `YOUTUBE_RESEARCH_WORKFLOW_TOKEN`.
- `timeout_seconds` - optional HTTP timeout. Defaults to `30`.
- `output_dir` - optional directory for generated files.
- `output_json` - optional explicit JSON output path.

## Outputs

The command returns normalized metadata as JSON and can write these files when
`--output-dir` is provided:

- `metadata.json`
- `source-url.txt`
- `result.json`

The normalized JSON includes:

- `video_id`
- `title`
- `author`
- `published_at`
- `youtube_url`
- `thumbnail`
- `source_url`
- `fetched_at`

## CLI

```bash
scripts/youtube-metadata-fetch.sh \
  --source-url "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dQw4w9WgXcQ" \
  --output-dir /tmp/youtube-metadata-output
```

With explicit API config:

```bash
scripts/youtube-metadata-fetch.sh \
  --api-url "$YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_URL" \
  --api-token "$YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_TOKEN" \
  --source-url "https://youtu.be/dQw4w9WgXcQ"
```

The CLI also loads private runtime configuration from these files when present:

- `<installed-skill>/.skill.env` generated by the installer for managed defaults.
- `~/.agent-brain-plugins.env`
- `~/agent-brain-plugins/youtube-wiki/env.shared`
- `~/.hermes/.env`

Runtime hosts can override installer defaults through private env files or the
Runtime/Instance secret layer.

## Runtime Node Guidance

A node built from this skill should treat `source_url` as a manual or first-node
input. If the node is downstream of another node, the edge handoff instruction
should decide whether and how to provide the URL. The skill itself must not make
that decision.

---
name: youtube-metadata-fetch
description: Fetch normalized metadata for a YouTube video URL through the configured YouTube content API. This base skill is workflow-neutral and does not know about SOP nodes, upstream nodes, downstream nodes, pipeline context, git, Telegram, or edge handoff mappings.
---

# YouTube Metadata Fetch

Use this skill when a runtime node or agent needs YouTube video metadata from a
source URL.

This is a base skill. It intentionally does not encode any SOP stage, node id,
upstream dependency, downstream dependency, edge handoff, git commit, Telegram
notification, or wiki repository contract.

## Install Command

```bash
bash <(curl -fsSL 'https://skill.vyibc.com/install-youtube-metadata-fetch.sh?ts=20260630100126')
```

## Inputs

- `source_url` - required YouTube watch or youtu.be URL.
- `api_url` - optional API base URL. Defaults to `YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_URL`, then the managed YouTube content API endpoint.
- `api_token` - optional bearer token. Defaults to `YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_TOKEN`, then `YOUTUBE_RESEARCH_WORKFLOW_TOKEN`.
- `timeout_seconds` - optional HTTP timeout. Defaults to `30`.
- `output_dir` - optional directory for generated files.
- `output_json` - optional explicit JSON output path.

## Outputs

The command returns normalized metadata as JSON and can write these files when
`--output-dir` is provided:

- `metadata.json`
- `source-url.txt`
- `result.json`

The normalized JSON includes:

- `video_id`
- `title`
- `author`
- `published_at`
- `youtube_url`
- `thumbnail`
- `source_url`
- `fetched_at`

## CLI

```bash
scripts/youtube-metadata-fetch.sh \
  --source-url "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dQw4w9WgXcQ" \
  --output-dir /tmp/youtube-metadata-output
```

With explicit API config:

```bash
scripts/youtube-metadata-fetch.sh \
  --api-url "$YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_URL" \
  --api-token "$YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_TOKEN" \
  --source-url "https://youtu.be/dQw4w9WgXcQ"
```

The CLI also loads private runtime configuration from these files when present:

- `<installed-skill>/.skill.env` generated by the installer for managed defaults.
- `~/.agent-brain-plugins.env`
- `~/agent-brain-plugins/youtube-wiki/env.shared`
- `~/.hermes/.env`

Runtime hosts can override installer defaults through private env files or the
Runtime/Instance secret layer.

## Runtime Node Guidance

A node built from this skill should treat `source_url` as a manual or first-node
input. If the node is downstream of another node, the edge handoff instruction
should decide whether and how to provide the URL. The skill itself must not make
that decision.

---
name: youtube-metadata-fetch
description: Fetch normalized metadata for a YouTube video URL through the configured YouTube content API. This base skill is workflow-neutral and does not know about SOP nodes, upstream nodes, downstream nodes, pipeline context, git, Telegram, or edge handoff mappings.
---

# YouTube Metadata Fetch

Use this skill when a runtime node or agent needs YouTube video metadata from a
source URL.

This is a base skill. It intentionally does not encode any SOP stage, node id,
upstream dependency, downstream dependency, edge handoff, git commit, Telegram
notification, or wiki repository contract.

## Install Command

```bash
bash <(curl -fsSL 'https://skill.vyibc.com/install-youtube-metadata-fetch.sh?ts=20260630100126')
```

## Inputs

- `source_url` - required YouTube watch or youtu.be URL.
- `api_url` - optional API base URL. Defaults to `YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_URL`, then the managed YouTube content API endpoint.
- `api_token` - optional bearer token. Defaults to `YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_TOKEN`, then `YOUTUBE_RESEARCH_WORKFLOW_TOKEN`.
- `timeout_seconds` - optional HTTP timeout. Defaults to `30`.
- `output_dir` - optional directory for generated files.
- `output_json` - optional explicit JSON output path.

## Outputs

The command returns normalized metadata as JSON and can write these files when
`--output-dir` is provided:

- `metadata.json`
- `source-url.txt`
- `result.json`

The normalized JSON includes:

- `video_id`
- `title`
- `author`
- `published_at`
- `youtube_url`
- `thumbnail`
- `source_url`
- `fetched_at`

## CLI

```bash
scripts/youtube-metadata-fetch.sh \
  --source-url "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dQw4w9WgXcQ" \
  --output-dir /tmp/youtube-metadata-output
```

With explicit API config:

```bash
scripts/youtube-metadata-fetch.sh \
  --api-url "$YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_URL" \
  --api-token "$YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_TOKEN" \
  --source-url "https://youtu.be/dQw4w9WgXcQ"
```

The CLI also loads private runtime configuration from these files when present:

- `<installed-skill>/.skill.env` generated by the installer for managed defaults.
- `~/.agent-brain-plugins.env`
- `~/agent-brain-plugins/youtube-wiki/env.shared`
- `~/.hermes/.env`

Runtime hosts can override installer defaults through private env files or the
Runtime/Instance secret layer.

## Runtime Node Guidance

A node built from this skill should treat `source_url` as a manual or first-node
input. If the node is downstream of another node, the edge handoff instruction
should decide whether and how to provide the URL. The skill itself must not make
that decision.

---
name: youtube-metadata-fetch
description: Fetch normalized metadata for a YouTube video URL through the configured YouTube content API. This base skill is workflow-neutral and does not know about SOP nodes, upstream nodes, downstream nodes, pipeline context, git, Telegram, or edge handoff mappings.
---

# YouTube Metadata Fetch

Use this skill when a runtime node or agent needs YouTube video metadata from a
source URL.

This is a base skill. It intentionally does not encode any SOP stage, node id,
upstream dependency, downstream dependency, edge handoff, git commit, Telegram
notification, or wiki repository contract.

## Install Command

```bash
bash <(curl -fsSL 'https://skill.vyibc.com/install-youtube-metadata-fetch.sh?ts=20260630100126')
```

## Inputs

- `source_url` - required YouTube watch or youtu.be URL.
- `api_url` - optional API base URL. Defaults to `YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_URL`, then the managed YouTube content API endpoint.
- `api_token` - optional bearer token. Defaults to `YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_TOKEN`, then `YOUTUBE_RESEARCH_WORKFLOW_TOKEN`.
- `timeout_seconds` - optional HTTP timeout. Defaults to `30`.
- `output_dir` - optional directory for generated files.
- `output_json` - optional explicit JSON output path.

## Outputs

The command returns normalized metadata as JSON and can write these files when
`--output-dir` is provided:

- `metadata.json`
- `source-url.txt`
- `result.json`

The normalized JSON includes:

- `video_id`
- `title`
- `author`
- `published_at`
- `youtube_url`
- `thumbnail`
- `source_url`
- `fetched_at`

## CLI

```bash
scripts/youtube-metadata-fetch.sh \
  --source-url "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dQw4w9WgXcQ" \
  --output-dir /tmp/youtube-metadata-output
```

With explicit API config:

```bash
scripts/youtube-metadata-fetch.sh \
  --api-url "$YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_URL" \
  --api-token "$YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_TOKEN" \
  --source-url "https://youtu.be/dQw4w9WgXcQ"
```

The CLI also loads private runtime configuration from these files when present:

- `<installed-skill>/.skill.env` generated by the installer for managed defaults.
- `~/.agent-brain-plugins.env`
- `~/agent-brain-plugins/youtube-wiki/env.shared`
- `~/.hermes/.env`

Runtime hosts can override installer defaults through private env files or the
Runtime/Instance secret layer.

## Runtime Node Guidance

A node built from this skill should treat `source_url` as a manual or first-node
input. If the node is downstream of another node, the edge handoff instruction
should decide whether and how to provide the URL. The skill itself must not make
that decision.

---
name: youtube-metadata-fetch
description: Fetch normalized metadata for a YouTube video URL through the configured YouTube content API. This base skill is workflow-neutral and does not know about SOP nodes, upstream nodes, downstream nodes, pipeline context, git, Telegram, or edge handoff mappings.
---

# YouTube Metadata Fetch

Use this skill when a runtime node or agent needs YouTube video metadata from a
source URL.

This is a base skill. It intentionally does not encode any SOP stage, node id,
upstream dependency, downstream dependency, edge handoff, git commit, Telegram
notification, or wiki repository contract.

## Install Command

```bash
bash <(curl -fsSL 'https://skill.vyibc.com/install-youtube-metadata-fetch.sh?ts=20260630100126')
```

## Inputs

- `source_url` - required YouTube watch or youtu.be URL.
- `api_url` - optional API base URL. Defaults to `YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_URL`, then the managed YouTube content API endpoint.
- `api_token` - optional bearer token. Defaults to `YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_TOKEN`, then `YOUTUBE_RESEARCH_WORKFLOW_TOKEN`.
- `timeout_seconds` - optional HTTP timeout. Defaults to `30`.
- `output_dir` - optional directory for generated files.
- `output_json` - optional explicit JSON output path.

## Outputs

The command returns normalized metadata as JSON and can write these files when
`--output-dir` is provided:

- `metadata.json`
- `source-url.txt`
- `result.json`

The normalized JSON includes:

- `video_id`
- `title`
- `author`
- `published_at`
- `youtube_url`
- `thumbnail`
- `source_url`
- `fetched_at`

## CLI

```bash
scripts/youtube-metadata-fetch.sh \
  --source-url "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dQw4w9WgXcQ" \
  --output-dir /tmp/youtube-metadata-output
```

With explicit API config:

```bash
scripts/youtube-metadata-fetch.sh \
  --api-url "$YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_URL" \
  --api-token "$YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_TOKEN" \
  --source-url "https://youtu.be/dQw4w9WgXcQ"
```

The CLI also loads private runtime configuration from these files when present:

- `<installed-skill>/.skill.env` generated by the installer for managed defaults.
- `~/.agent-brain-plugins.env`
- `~/agent-brain-plugins/youtube-wiki/env.shared`
- `~/.hermes/.env`

Runtime hosts can override installer defaults through private env files or the
Runtime/Instance secret layer.

## Runtime Node Guidance

A node built from this skill should treat `source_url` as a manual or first-node
input. If the node is downstream of another node, the edge handoff instruction
should decide whether and how to provide the URL. The skill itself must not make
that decision.

---
name: youtube-metadata-fetch
description: Fetch normalized metadata for a YouTube video URL through the configured YouTube content API. This base skill is workflow-neutral and does not know about SOP nodes, upstream nodes, downstream nodes, pipeline context, git, Telegram, or edge handoff mappings.
---

# YouTube Metadata Fetch

Use this skill when a runtime node or agent needs YouTube video metadata from a
source URL.

This is a base skill. It intentionally does not encode any SOP stage, node id,
upstream dependency, downstream dependency, edge handoff, git commit, Telegram
notification, or wiki repository contract.

## Install Command

```bash
bash <(curl -fsSL 'https://skill.vyibc.com/install-youtube-metadata-fetch.sh?ts=20260630100126')
```

## Inputs

- `source_url` - required YouTube watch or youtu.be URL.
- `api_url` - optional API base URL. Defaults to `YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_URL`, then the managed YouTube content API endpoint.
- `api_token` - optional bearer token. Defaults to `YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_TOKEN`, then `YOUTUBE_RESEARCH_WORKFLOW_TOKEN`.
- `timeout_seconds` - optional HTTP timeout. Defaults to `30`.
- `output_dir` - optional directory for generated files.
- `output_json` - optional explicit JSON output path.

## Outputs

The command returns normalized metadata as JSON and can write these files when
`--output-dir` is provided:

- `metadata.json`
- `source-url.txt`
- `result.json`

The normalized JSON includes:

- `video_id`
- `title`
- `author`
- `published_at`
- `youtube_url`
- `thumbnail`
- `source_url`

---
name: youtube-metadata-fetch
description: Fetch normalized metadata for a YouTube video URL through the configured YouTube content API. This base skill is workflow-neutral and does not know about SOP nodes, upstream nodes, downstream nodes, pipeline context, git, Telegram, or edge handoff mappings.
---

# YouTube Metadata Fetch

Use this skill when a runtime node or agent needs YouTube video metadata from a
source URL.

This is a base skill. It intentionally does not encode any SOP stage, node id,
upstream dependency, downstream dependency, edge handoff, git commit, Telegram
notification, or wiki repository contract.

## Install Command

```bash
bash <(curl -fsSL 'https://skill.vyibc.com/install-youtube-metadata-fetch.sh?ts=20260630100126')
```

## Inputs

- `source_url` - required YouTube watch or youtu.be URL.
- `api_url` - optional API base URL. Defaults to `YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_URL`, then the managed YouTube content API endpoint.
- `api_token` - optional bearer token. Defaults to `YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_TOKEN`, then `YOUTUBE_RESEARCH_WORKFLOW_TOKEN`.
- `timeout_seconds` - optional HTTP timeout. Defaults to `30`.
- `output_dir` - optional directory for generated files.
- `output_json` - optional explicit JSON output path.

## Outputs

The command returns normalized metadata as JSON and can write these files when
`--output-dir` is provided:

- `metadata.json`
- `source-url.txt`
- `result.json`

The normalized JSON includes:

- `video_id`
- `title`
- `author`
- `published_at`
- `youtube_url`
- `thumbnail`
- `source_url`
- `fetched_at`

## CLI

```bash
scripts/youtube-metadata-fetch.sh \
  --source-url "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dQw4w9WgXcQ" \
  --output-dir /tmp/youtube-metadata-output
```

With explicit API config:

```bash
scripts/youtube-metadata-fetch.sh \
  --api-url "$YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_URL" \
  --api-token "$YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_TOKEN" \
  --source-url "https://youtu.be/dQw4w9WgXcQ"
```

The CLI also loads private runtime configuration from these files when present:

- `<installed-skill>/.skill.env` generated by the installer for managed defaults.
- `~/.agent-brain-plugins.env`
- `~/agent-brain-plugins/youtube-wiki/env.shared`
- `~/.hermes/.env`

Runtime hosts can override installer defaults through private env files or the
Runtime/Instance secret layer.

## Runtime Node Guidance

A node built from this skill should treat `source_url` as a manual or first-node
input. If the node is downstream of another node, the edge handoff instruction
should decide whether and how to provide the URL. The skill itself must not make
that decision.

---
name: youtube-metadata-fetch
description: Fetch normalized metadata for a YouTube video URL through the configured YouTube content API. This base skill is workflow-neutral and does not know about SOP nodes, upstream nodes, downstream nodes, pipeline context, git, Telegram, or edge handoff mappings.
---

# YouTube Metadata Fetch

Use this skill when a runtime node or agent needs YouTube video metadata from a
source URL.

This is a base skill. It intentionally does not encode any SOP stage, node id,
upstream dependency, downstream dependency, edge handoff, git commit, Telegram
notification, or wiki repository contract.

## Install Command

```bash
bash <(curl -fsSL 'https://skill.vyibc.com/install-youtube-metadata-fetch.sh?ts=20260630100126')
```

## Inputs

- `source_url` - required YouTube watch or youtu.be URL.
- `api_url` - optional API base URL. Defaults to `YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_URL`, then the managed YouTube content API endpoint.
- `api_token` - optional bearer token. Defaults to `YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_TOKEN`, then `YOUTUBE_RESEARCH_WORKFLOW_TOKEN`.
- `timeout_seconds` - optional HTTP timeout. Defaults to `30`.
- `output_dir` - optional directory for generated files.
- `output_json` - optional explicit JSON output path.

## Outputs

The command returns normalized metadata as JSON and can write these files when
`--output-dir` is provided:

- `metadata.json`
- `source-url.txt`
- `result.json`

The normalized JSON includes:

- `video_id`
- `title`
- `author`
- `published_at`
- `youtube_url`
- `thumbnail`
- `source_url`
- `fetched_at`

## CLI

```bash
scripts/youtube-metadata-fetch.sh \
  --source-url "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dQw4w9WgXcQ" \
  --output-dir /tmp/youtube-metadata-output
```

With explicit API config:

```bash
scripts/youtube-metadata-fetch.sh \
  --api-url "$YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_URL" \
  --api-token "$YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_TOKEN" \
  --source-url "https://youtu.be/dQw4w9WgXcQ"
```

The CLI also loads private runtime configuration from these files when present:

- `<installed-skill>/.skill.env` generated by the installer for managed defaults.
- `~/.agent-brain-plugins.env`
- `~/agent-brain-plugins/youtube-wiki/env.shared`
- `~/.hermes/.env`

Runtime hosts can override installer defaults through private env files or the
Runtime/Instance secret layer.

## Runtime Node Guidance

A node built from this skill should treat `source_url` as a manual or first-node
input. If the node is downstream of another node, the edge handoff instruction
should decide whether and how to provide the URL. The skill itself must not make
that decision.

---
name: youtube-metadata-fetch
description: Fetch normalized metadata for a YouTube video URL through the configured YouTube content API. This base skill is workflow-neutral and does not know about SOP nodes, upstream nodes, downstream nodes, pipeline context, git, Telegram, or edge handoff mappings.
---

# YouTube Metadata Fetch

Use this skill when a runtime node or agent needs YouTube video metadata from a
source URL.

This is a base skill. It intentionally does not encode any SOP stage, node id,
upstream dependency, downstream dependency, edge handoff, git commit, Telegram
notification, or wiki repository contract.

## Install Command

```bash
bash <(curl -fsSL 'https://skill.vyibc.com/install-youtube-metadata-fetch.sh?ts=20260630100126')
```

## Inputs

- `source_url` - required YouTube watch or youtu.be URL.
- `api_url` - optional API base URL. Defaults to `YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_URL`, then the managed YouTube content API endpoint.
- `api_token` - optional bearer token. Defaults to `YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_TOKEN`, then `YOUTUBE_RESEARCH_WORKFLOW_TOKEN`.
- `timeout_seconds` - optional HTTP timeout. Defaults to `30`.
- `output_dir` - optional directory for generated files.
- `output_json` - optional explicit JSON output path.

## Outputs

The command returns normalized metadata as JSON and can write these files when
`--output-dir` is provided:

- `metadata.json`
- `source-url.txt`
- `result.json`

The normalized JSON includes:

- `video_id`
- `title`
- `author`
- `published_at`
- `youtube_url`
- `thumbnail`
- `source_url`
- `fetched_at`

## CLI

```bash
scripts/youtube-metadata-fetch.sh \
  --source-url "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dQw4w9WgXcQ" \
  --output-dir /tmp/youtube-metadata-output
```

With explicit API config:

```bash
scripts/youtube-metadata-fetch.sh \
  --api-url "$YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_URL" \
  --api-token "$YOUTUBE_CONTENT_API_TOKEN" \
  --source-url "https://youtu.be/dQw4w9WgXcQ"
```

The CLI also loads private runtime configuration from these files when present:

- `<installed-skill>/.skill.env` generated by the installer for managed defaults.
- `~/.agent-brain-plugins.env`
- `~/agent-brain-plugins/youtube-wiki/env.shared`
- `~/.hermes/.env`

Runtime hosts can override installer defaults through private env files or the
Runtime/Instance secret layer.

## Runtime Node Guidance

A node built from this skill should treat `source_url` as a manual or first-node
input. If the node is downstream of another node, the edge handoff instruction
should decide whether and how to provide the URL. The skill itself must not make
that decision.
